_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-19 Refusal of Care (Revised 3/24/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-19: Refusal of Care — MDHHS Approved 3/24/2023
purpose: Govern EMS response when a patient or guardian refuses care or transport. Capacity to refuse must be assessed. Minors cannot consent to or refuse EMS care. Religious belief exceptions apply. Thorough documentation is mandatory.

sections.title: Assessing Decision-Making Capacity
points: A patient has decision-making capacity (CAPABLE) if ALL of the following are present:
1. Patient is awake and alert
2. Patient is oriented to person, place, time, and situation
3. Patient understands the risks of refusing care (including potential for serious harm or death)
4. Patient is ≥ 18 years old OR is an emancipated minor
5. Patient is NOT under the influence of alcohol or drugs to a degree that impairs judgment
6. Patient is NOT a danger to self or others
EMANCIPATED MINOR criteria (any one qualifies): married; active duty military member; court-granted emancipation

sections.title: Capable Patient Refusal
points: Explain the nature of the condition and the potential consequences of refusing care, including the risk of serious injury or death
Document the explanation given and the patient's demonstrated understanding
Obtain the patient's signature on the EMS Refusal Form
If patient refuses to sign: obtain witness signature(s) on the form and document the circumstances
Strongly encourage the patient to call back if symptoms worsen or change
Inform the patient they may call 9-1-1 again at any time
Document all findings, the explanation provided, and the patient's decision in the PCR

sections.title: Incapable Patient — Unable to Refuse
points: A patient who lacks decision-making capacity CANNOT legally refuse care
Contact Medical Control and treat/transport for any serious illness or injury using implied consent
Document the basis for determining the patient lacks capacity
Law enforcement involvement may be needed for safety if patient is combative

sections.title: Capable Patient After Treatment
points: If a capable patient refuses transport AFTER receiving treatment:
Contact Medical Control if medications were administered
Strongly encourage hospital transport — explain risks of refusing post-treatment
Document the medications given, Medical Control contact, explanation of risks, and the patient's decision

sections.title: Minors
points: Minors (< 18 years; not emancipated) CANNOT consent to or refuse EMS care
Parent or legal guardian consent is required for non-emergency treatment of a minor
Do NOT delay life-threatening care to obtain consent for a minor
If a parent is refusing care for a minor: contact Medical Control; provide care and transport for life-threatening conditions; involve law enforcement if needed
Document all attempts to obtain consent and the clinical basis for providing care without consent

sections.title: Religious Beliefs Exception
points: Capable adults may refuse treatment based on religious or personal beliefs even if the refusal may lead to serious harm or death
This exception applies even if the patient meets criteria that would otherwise suggest incapacity based solely on medical condition
Document the religious or personal belief-based refusal thoroughly
Contact Medical Control

deterioration_flags: Incapable patient attempting to refuse — document basis for incapacity; treat and transport under implied consent; contact MC
Minor with life-threatening condition and parent refusing — provide care and transport; contact MC; involve law enforcement if needed
Post-medication refusal — contact MC before releasing patient; document all medications given and patient response
scope_notes: Capable refusal criteria: awake + oriented + understands risks + ≥ 18 yr (or emancipated) + not impaired + not danger to self/others
Emancipated minor: married, active duty military, or court-granted
Incapable patient: treat/transport under implied consent; contact MC
Minor: cannot consent or refuse; parent/guardian required; do not delay life-threatening care
Religious belief: exception to capacity-based refusal even if refusal may cause serious harm
Documentation required in all refusal cases regardless of outcome
